description: IraGo Air Mobility Platform MVP - Express + Prisma + MySQL backend with bcrypt auth, plus customer, operator, and admin experiences served from a single app.html gated by role. Fare, live position, pre-flight checklist, and payments are mocked for now; the data layer (users, bookings, assignments, status) is real and persisted.

userStories.title: Add Express + Prisma + MySQL backend skeleton
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Express server with a Prisma/MySQL connection so the app has a real API and database, following the Hostinger Node.js + MySQL setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Add dependencies: express, prisma, @prisma/client, bcrypt, dotenv
server.js (or new app.js) serves existing static files AND mounts an /api router via express.static
Database is selected by env vars: DATABASE_PROVIDER (mysql default -> Hostinger, or sqlite for local testing) and DATABASE_URL (connection string)

Prisma schema datasource uses DATABASE_URL; provider switches between mysql and sqlite based on DATABASE_PROVIDER (use a schema-swap setup script or equivalent since Prisma provider must be a literal). Default/production = Hostinger MySQL per the Hostinger guide; local = file:./dev.db SQLite
Commit .env.example documenting both DATABASE_PROVIDER and DATABASE_URL with the Hostinger MySQL default plus a commented local SQLite override; real .env is gitignored
npm start boots the server with a working DB connection and fails loudly on bad credentials
GET /api/health returns { status: 'ok', db: 'connected' }
Document Hostinger connection steps, the env-var DB switch, and required env vars in a README or notes file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Express 5 + Prisma 6 (pinned; Prisma 7 dropped url=env datasource). server.js serves static + /api; src/db.js Prisma singleton; src/api.js has GET /api/health -> {status:ok,db:connected}. Provider swapped via scripts/db-setup.js (DATABASE_PROVIDER). Verified locally on SQLite: health ok, static served, fails loudly (exit 1) on bad DB. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Create the database schema (users, bookings, aircraft, trips) + seed
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Prisma schema so all app data persists with the right relationships and seeded sample data.
acceptanceCriteria: User model: id, name, email (unique), passwordHash, role enum customer|operator|admin, timestamps
Aircraft model: id, name/tail number, model, status enum available|in_flight|maintenance, capacity

Booking model: id, customerId FK->User, pickupName/pickupLat/pickupLng, destName/destLat/destLng, service, distanceKm, fareEstimate, status enum requested|assigned|accepted|rejected|enroute|picked_up|flying|arrived|completed|cancelled, timestamps
Booking has optional operatorId FK->User and aircraftId FK->Aircraft for assignment
Prisma migration generates and applies cleanly against the env-selected database (SQLite locally, MySQL on Hostinger); avoid DB-specific column types so the schema works on both
Seed script inserts 1 admin, 2 operators, 2 customers, and 3 aircraft
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: User/Aircraft/Booking models added to prisma/schema.prisma. role/status are String columns (Prisma enums unsupported on SQLite) with allowed values documented + enforced in app code; no DB-specific types so it works on SQLite + MySQL. Booking has optional operatorId/aircraftId FKs + indexes. prisma/seed.js (idempotent upserts) seeds 1 admin, 2 operators, 2 customers, 3 aircraft, all bcrypt-hashed (demo pw password123). Added db:seed script. Migrations are dialect-specific under the provider swap, so not committed; db:push applies the schema. Verified on SQLite: db:push synced, seed counts correct, re-seed idempotent, typecheck green. Committed schema provider restored to mysql.

userStories.title: Signup, login, and session token API with bcrypt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need auth endpoints so users can register and log in securely, with passwords hashed one-way so they can never be stolen in plaintext.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/signup accepts name, email, password (role defaults to customer) and rejects duplicate email
Passwords stored only as a bcrypt hash; plaintext is never persisted or returned
POST /api/auth/login verifies email + password via bcrypt.compare and returns a session token plus user id, name, and role (never the hash)
Invalid credentials return a generic 401 with no field-level leak
Auth middleware validates the token on protected routes and exposes the current user + role
requireRole(role) guard rejects wrong-role requests with 403
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/auth.js: bcrypt hashPassword/verifyPassword + stateless HMAC-SHA256 signed tokens (signToken/verifyToken, dependency-free via node crypto; secret from AUTH_SECRET with insecure dev fallback + warning; 7-day exp; constant-time sig check). requireAuth middleware validates Bearer token -> req.user={id,name,role}; requireRole(role) -> 403 on mismatch. src/auth-routes.js mounted at /api/auth: POST /signup (name/email/password, role forced to customer, 409 on dup email, emails lowercased, never returns/persists plaintext), POST /login (bcrypt.compare, generic 401 for bad email OR password, returns token+public user no hash). GET /api/me protected by requireAuth. test/auth.test.js: 12 DB-free node:test cases (hashing, token roundtrip/tamper/expiry/garbage, requireAuth/requireRole) all pass via `npm test` (added test script). Verified live on SQLite: signup 201, dup 409, login 200 w/ token, case-insensitive email, bad pw 401, /me with token returns user, /me without token 401. Typecheck green.

userStories.title: Wire signup + login UI to the auth API
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to create an account and log in so my bookings are tied to me.
acceptanceCriteria: The existing login-view supports both login and signup (name/email/password) and calls the real API
On success the session token is stored client-side and the role-appropriate view loads (customer -> booking view)
Errors (duplicate email, wrong password) show a clear inline message
Logout clears the token and returns to login
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: app.html login-view wired to the real auth API. Login card -> POST /api/auth/login, register card -> POST /api/auth/signup (both inline in app.html). Added a client AUTH helper (localStorage token+user under irago_token/irago_user), apiFetch wrapper (adds Bearer header, auto-logout on 401), showView/routeForRole (customer -> booking-view + initMap; operator/admin fall through to booking-view until US-008/US-013), onAuthSuccess, logout (clears token, returns to login; wired to nav-avatar click), and restoreSession on DOMContentLoaded (GET /api/me). Inline .auth-error shown for empty fields, bad creds (generic 401 msg), duplicate email (409), <6 char pw, and network errors. Google button is a stub (googleStub) showing 'not available yet'. Enter key submits. Verified: typecheck green; inline JS node --check OK; headless DOM harness against live server passed all 6 flows (empty->error, bad pw->error+stay, good login->token+booking view+avatar 'C'+map init, logout->cleared+login, dup signup->error, /me restore->booking view). No browser MCP available, so visual browser check still pending manual confirmation.

userStories.title: Pickup, destination, and service selection captured for booking
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to enter pickup and destination and pick a service so the system knows my trip.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing map pickup/destination entry and service selector remain functional
Selected pickup/destination (name + lat/lng), service, and computed distance are captured for booking creation
Distance is computed (existing client calc is fine) and passed to the fare step
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added a structured bookingDraft state object in app.html ({pickup:{name,lat,lng}, dest:{name,lat,lng}, service, distanceKm}) as the single source of truth for the in-progress booking. captureBookingDraft() reads pickupCoord/destCoord + input names + currentService + calcDistance() into it; called from setPickup, setDest, swapLocations, switchService, and searchRides (the fare step now uses draft.distanceKm). bookingDraftReady() gates on pickup+dest+service all set (for US-006). resetBooking() clears the draft. distanceKm stays null until BOTH ends are chosen. Existing map entry, autocomplete, popular-route chips, and service tabs remain functional. SERVICE_LABELS map added for display/persistence. Verified: typecheck green; node --check on inline JS OK; npm test 12/12; headless vm+stub harness covered all 7 flows (empty not ready, pickup-only captured + dist null, dest -> ready + distance, service switch retains distance, swap swaps names/coords, reset clears, searchRides captures). No browser MCP in env, so visual browser check remains manual.

userStories.title: Fare estimate (mock) and booking confirmation
userStories.description: As a customer, I want a fare estimate and a confirm step so I can book a flight.
acceptanceCriteria: Fare estimate shown via a mock pricing function (base + per-km by service) isolated in one place clearly marked as mock-to-be-replaced
Confirming calls POST /api/bookings, persisting a Booking with status requested tied to the logged-in customer
Confirmation screen reflects the saved booking (id, route, service, fare)
A booking cannot be created unless pickup, destination, and service are set
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Mock pricing isolated in src/pricing.js (clearly marked MOCK — TO BE REPLACED): estimateFare(service, distanceKm) = base + perKm by service (taxi/golden/shuttle), rounded to ₹100; also exports haversineKm so the server computes distance authoritatively (never trusts client geometry/money). POST /api/bookings (src/booking-routes.js, mounted in api.js, requireAuth) validates pickup+dest (name+coords) and a valid service, computes distanceKm + fareEstimate server-side, persists Booking status 'requested' tied to req.user.id, returns {booking}. app.html bookRide() is now async: re-captures bookingDraft, gates on bookingDraftReady() (client mirror of server guard), POSTs via apiFetch (no fare sent — server-authoritative), then fillConfirmation(booking) populates the overlay from the SAVED booking (id->IRG-00007, pickup/dest names, SERVICE_LABELS[service], ₹fareEstimate). currentBooking global stores the saved record for US-007 tracking; resetBooking clears it. Inline #booking-error for gate/server/network failures. test/pricing.test.js (8 cases). Verified: typecheck green; npm test 19/19; node --check on inline JS OK; live SQLite API — valid POST 201 (status requested, customerId from token, distanceKm 14.1, fare 8500), missing service/coords 400, no auth 401, bad service 400; headless vm harness — not-ready gated (no POST), ready POSTs + confirmation reflects saved booking + currentBooking set, server 400 -> inline error no overlay. Visual browser check manual (no browser MCP).

userStories.title: Booking status tracking + live tracking view (mock position)
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to track my booking's status so I know what's happening.
acceptanceCriteria: Tracking view loads the booking's real status from GET /api/bookings/:id via polling
Status steps (confirmed -> enroute -> pickup -> flying -> arrived) reflect the persisted booking status set by the operator
Aircraft position/ETA on the map is mocked (animated) and clearly marked as placeholder for real GPS
If no active booking exists, the view shows an appropriate empty state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added GET /api/bookings/:id (src/booking-routes.js, requireAuth) returning the persisted booking; readable by the owning customer, assigned operator, or admin (403 otherwise, 404 missing, 400 bad id). app.html tracking view rewritten: the time-based simulation is replaced by REAL status polling — startTracking() polls GET /api/bookings/:id every 4s and applyTrackingStatus(status) maps the persisted status to the 5 step dots (confirmed/enroute/pickup/flying/arrived) + progress bar + copy. STATUS_DISPLAY maps all DB statuses (requested/assigned/accepted->confirmed, enroute, picked_up->pickup, flying, arrived/completed->arrived, rejected/cancelled->red dot). Aircraft position + ETA stay a MOCK animation (animateTrackingAircraft eases marker toward status-implied route fraction), clearly marked TO BE REPLACED with real GPS. Empty state (#tracking-empty) shown when no currentBooking. Polling stops at terminal statuses (arrived/completed/cancelled/rejected). endTracking clears both intervals. Verified: typecheck green; npm test 19/19; node --check on inline JS OK; live SQLite API — GET own 200, admin 200, other-customer 403, no-token 401, missing 404, bad-id 400, and operator-set status (DB flying) reflected by GET; vm+stub harness 14/14 (empty state, status->step mapping for requested/enroute/flying/arrived/cancelled, progress %, dot colors, poll picks up new status). Visual browser check manual (no browser MCP).

userStories.title: Operator login routes to the operator views
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to log in and land on my pilot dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: A user with role operator logging in is shown the operator view (not the customer booking view)
Operator views live in the same app.html, shown/hidden by role
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added a role-gated operator (Pilot Console) landing view in app.html. New `#operator-view` .view (nav + 'Pilot Console' badge + personalized welcome + 'Assigned Trips' section with an empty-state placeholder that US-009 will replace) plus an OPERATOR DASHBOARD CSS block. routeForRole() now switches on role: operator -> showView('operator-view') + sets #op-welcome to 'Welcome back, <name>' + #op-avatar initial; customer/default -> booking-view + initMap (admin still falls through to booking-view until US-013). Operator avatar has its own id (#op-avatar) wired to logout(); booking-view keeps #nav-avatar. Verified: typecheck green; npm test 19/19; node --check on extracted inline JS OK; vm+stub harness 8/8 (operator->operator-view only + personalized welcome + avatar; customer->booking-view + 'C'; admin->booking-view fallback; operator switch hides booking-view); live SQLite API login — olivia@irago.test returns role 'operator' (+token), casey@irago.test returns 'customer'. No browser MCP, so visual browser check remains manual.

userStories.title: Assigned trip list + trip details
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to see trips assigned to me and open their details.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/operator/trips returns bookings assigned to the logged-in operator
List shows route, customer, service, status, and assigned aircraft
Selecting a trip opens a details view with full pickup/destination, distance, fare, and customer info
Empty state shown when no trips are assigned
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: GET /api/operator/trips added in new src/operator-routes.js (mounted at /api/operator in api.js), guarded by requireAuth + requireRole('operator'). Returns bookings where operatorId === req.user.id, newest first, with customer (id/name/email — NEVER passwordHash, via select) and aircraft (id/name/model/status) included. app.html operator-view rebuilt: op-body now has #op-list-section (welcome + #op-trips list) and a hidden #op-detail-section. loadOperatorTrips() (called from routeForRole operator case) fetches via apiFetch and renderOperatorTrips() paints cards (route, customer, service label, aircraft, status badge) or the empty state; openTripDetails(id) renders the full details (IRG-ref, pickup/dest names+coords, distance, fare, aircraft, customer name+email) from the cached trip and toggles sections; closeTripDetails() returns to the list. Added STATUS_BADGE map, escapeHtml (all DB text escaped before innerHTML), bookingRef/fmtINR helpers + trip-card/details CSS. Verified: typecheck green; npm test 19/19; node --check on inline JS OK; live SQLite API — operator 200 (empty then 1 after assign, customer+aircraft included, no passwordHash), customer 403, no-token 401, other operator sees 0 (scoped by operatorId); vm+stub UI harness 18/18 (empty state, list route/customer/service/aircraft/badges, unassigned-aircraft, openTripDetails fields + section toggle, closeTripDetails, XSS escaping). No browser MCP, so visual browser check remains manual.

userStories.title: Accept / reject an assigned mission
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to accept or reject an assigned trip so dispatch knows if I'll fly it.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept sets booking status to accepted; reject sets it to rejected and frees it for reassignment
Actions call POST /api/operator/trips/:id/accept and /reject with operator role check
The customer's tracking view and the admin dashboard reflect the change
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: POST /api/operator/trips/:id/accept and /reject added to src/operator-routes.js (requireAuth + requireRole('operator')). Shared loadOwnTrip() re-checks booking.operatorId === req.user.id on the loaded record (400 bad id, 404 missing/not-yours). accept: only status 'assigned' -> 'accepted' (else 409). reject: only 'assigned' -> 'rejected' AND clears operatorId + aircraftId so dispatch can reassign it (US-015), else 409. Customer tracking already reflects the change (STATUS_DISPLAY maps accepted->confirmed, rejected->red dot; no client change for the tracking view). app.html: openTripDetails now renders Accept mission/Reject buttons (tripActionsHtml, only when status==='assigned') + an inline #op-action-error; acceptTrip/rejectTrip -> actOnTrip(id,action) POSTs via apiFetch, disables buttons, then reloads loadOperatorTrips() and reopens details (rejected trip is gone from the list -> falls back to closeTripDetails). Added .op-actions/.op-btn(--accept/--reject)/.op-action-error CSS. Verified: typecheck green; npm test 19/19; node --check on inline JS OK; live SQLite API 12/12 (accept->200 accepted, re-accept->409, reject->200 rejected+freed+persisted, other operator->404 scoped, customer->403, no token->401, bad id->400, missing->404, customer GET /api/bookings/:id sees accepted); vm+stub UI harness 10/10 (assigned shows both buttons, accept POSTs+reloads+buttons gone, reject POSTs+removed from list+returns to list, accepted shows no buttons). Admin dashboard reflection is deferred to US-014 (admin views not built yet); the persisted status change is what those views will read. Visual browser check manual (no browser MCP).

userStories.title: Operator status update actions
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to push status updates (enroute, picked up, flying, arrived, completed) so the customer and admin see live progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Operator can advance the trip through the status sequence via buttons
Each update persists via POST /api/operator/trips/:id/status and is reflected in the customer tracking view
Statuses can only move forward in a sensible order (no skipping backward)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Basic pre-flight checklist (mock)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a pre-flight checklist before starting a flight.
acceptanceCriteria: A checklist of items (battery, weather, weight, comms) is shown before moving the trip to flying
Implementation is mock/UI-only and clearly marked for future real integration
Cannot mark flying until all items are checked (client-side gate)

Assigned trip then appears in that operator's list
Only available aircraft and operators are selectable

Active trip monitor shows in-progress bookings with current status and assigned pilot/aircraft
View reflects status changes pushed by operators

Metrics come from real persisted data via GET /api/admin/reports
Numbers update on refresh
